aliases: 芝士玉米条 (Zhī Shì Yù Mǐ Tiáo)

chinese: 芝士玉米条 (Zhī Shì Yù Mǐ Tiáo)

region_of_origin: Singapore (manufactured by Jack 'n Jill / Universal Robina Corporation; originally developed in Australia)
food_subgroup: Puffed and extruded corn snack with artificial cheese flavouring; ultra-processed packaged snack food

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not cover branded corn snack products. Closest parent would be FOODON:03301710 (corn snack food product) but no exact URI confirmed; null used to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Corn snacks (extruded/puffed) — FoodEx2 category for puffed/extruded corn-based snack products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A039K covers extruded/puffed corn snacks as a category; no branded Twisties-specific code exists. Closest defensible parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for branded corn snack products.

preparation_method: flavour coating with artificial cheese powder
allergens: milk derivatives (artificial cheese flavouring may contain milk-derived components)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard retail pack of Twisties Cheese weighs 60g and is typically consumed in a single sitting, providing approximately 330 kcal, 420mg sodium, and 15g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Twisties Cheese Corn Snack — Jack 'n Jill Singapore Nutrition Information Panel (product label)
nutrient_source.organization: Universal Robina Corporation / Jack 'n Jill Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) as declared on the Jack 'n Jill Twisties Cheese 60g retail pack. Values are per 100g as declared. This is a USDA Branded Food category equivalent for a packaged commercial product; the manufacturer NIP is the primary and most authoritative source for this branded product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Puffed and Extruded Corn Snack Products — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~70 (high) assigned based on University of Sydney GI Database values for puffed and extruded corn snack products. No direct GI test exists for Twisties Cheese Singapore specifically; the value is an analogous estimate from the closest tested category (puffed/extruded corn snacks, GI range 67–74). Glycemic load per 60g serving = (70 × 46.0g available carbohydrate per 60g) / 100 = 32.2. Available carbohydrate per 60g = 76.7 × 0.60 = 46.02g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (700mg/100g; 420mg per 60g pack = 21% of the 2,000mg/day recommended limit)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid Twisties Cheese as a regular snack; a single 60g pack delivers 420mg sodium, and the light texture and strong artificial flavour promote overconsumption, making sodium intake difficult to control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and very high glycemic load per pack (~32), combined with negligible dietary fibre (1g/100g) and high energy density (550 kcal/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Twisties Cheese is not suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes; the high-GI puffed corn base causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation, the GL of ~32 per standard pack far exceeds the low-GL threshold of 10, and the near-zero fibre content provides no glycaemic buffering.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content (11g/100g; 6.6g per 60g pack) from palm oil, combined with high sodium (700mg/100g) and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Twisties Cheese should be avoided by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; the saturated fat from palm oil (11g/100g) raises LDL cholesterol, the high sodium load elevates blood pressure, and the ultra-processed nature of the product is independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (pro-inflammatory), high sodium, ultra-processed formulation with no anti-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Twisties Cheese is not recommended for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the high saturated fat from palm oil and the ultra-processed formulation may promote systemic inflammation, while the absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, antioxidants, fibre) provides no benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Twisties Cheese Flavoured Corn Snack is one of Singapore's most iconic packaged snack foods, manufactured by Jack 'n Jill (Universal Robina Corporation) and sold in a standard 60g retail pack. The product is made by extruding corn meal under high pressure and temperature to create a light, airy, twisted puff shape, which is then coated with an artificial cheddar-style cheese flavour powder. Despite the name, the product contains no real cheese — the flavour is entirely synthetic. The texture is crispy and highly friable, dissolving quickly in the mouth, which contributes to low satiety and easy overconsumption. Nutritionally, Twisties Cheese is a high-energy-density (550 kcal/100g), high-GI (GI ~70), high-sodium (700mg/100g), high-saturated-fat (11g/100g from palm oil) ultra-processed snack with negligible dietary fibre (1g/100g) and no meaningful micronutrient contribution. A single 60g pack delivers approximately 330 kcal, 420mg sodium, 15g fat (6.6g saturated), and 46g rapidly-digested carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~32 — well above the low-GL threshold. Clinically, Twisties is contraindicated as a regular snack for patients with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. Culturally, Twisties Cheese is deeply embedded in Singaporean childhood nostalgia, school canteen culture, birthday parties, and convenience store snacking. The product is halal-certified and vegetarian-suitable.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the manufacturer's Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) as declared on the Jack 'n Jill Twisties Cheese 60g retail pack — the most authoritative source for a branded packaged product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for puffed/extruded corn snacks (confidence 0.9), which is the approved GI source. All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed from the NIP.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not declared on the Singapore product NIP and is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific branded product as of April 2026. 2. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content is not declared on the product NIP and is not available from approved sources for this specific product. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per product NIP declaration). 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per product NIP; product contains no animal-derived fat — palm oil is the fat source). 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0 (confirmed zero or not present per product NIP; this is an ultra-processed corn snack with no added vitamins A, C, D, or B12). 6. GI value of 70 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for puffed/extruded corn snacks; no direct GI test for Twisties Cheese Singapore exists in the published literature. 7. FoodEx2 code A039K used as closest defensible parent for extruded/puffed corn snacks; no branded Twisties-specific FoodEx2 code exists. 8. The diabetes health_suitability entry in the input JSON incorrectly marked suitable=true for diabetes — this has been corrected to concern_level=high in the output, consistent with the clinical rationale provided in the input record itself.
